name: Create images from spreadsheet data with Layerre

parameters.content:
## Create Images from Spreadsheet Data with Layerre

This workflow automates the creation of personalized images at scale using Layerre.

### How it works
1. **Create Template**: First, create a Layerre template from your Canva design
2. **Load Data**: Get rows from the [public Google Sheets example](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1x8X_qZaoXkrIKGVlhCC25du8ht42UKinRPtmjThpTSE/edit?usp=sharing) with columns **Text** and **Image** (one row = one variant)
3. **Generate Variants**: Loop through each row and create image variants
4. **Output**: Get back the rendered image URLs for each variant

### Prerequisites
- A [Layerre account](https://layerre.com) with an API key
- A Canva design with the layers you want to customize
- A spreadsheet with your data (one row per variant)

### Customization
- The example sheet has columns **Text** and **Image**. Match your Canva layer IDs in the Create Variant node (e.g. `text`, `image`).
- Add more layer overrides in the "Create Variant" node
- Add downstream nodes to save images, send them via email, post to social media, etc.

### Resources
- [Layerre Documentation](https://layerre.com/docs)
- [Layerre n8n Node Documentation](https://github.com/layerre/n8n-nodes-layerre)
- [n8n Documentation](https://docs.n8n.io)

## Step 1: Create Template

Create a Layerre template from your Canva design. This only needs to be done once.

**Instructions:**
1. Share your Canva design (click Share → "Anyone with the link can view")
2. Paste the Canva URL in the node below
3. Execute this node to create the template

**Note:** You can deactivate this node after the template is created since you only need to create it once.

## Step 2: Get Rows from Google Sheet

**Instructions:**
1. Add Google Sheets credentials (OAuth2 or Service Account) if you haven't already
2. Select your spreadsheet: choose from list or paste the spreadsheet ID from the URL
3. Select the sheet (tab) to read from
4. Operation **Get Row(s)** returns every row (first row = headers). Each data row becomes one variant.

**Using the public Google Sheets example:** This workflow is configured to read from the public [Layerre Sheets Example](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1x8X_qZaoXkrIKGVlhCC25du8ht42UKinRPtmjThpTSE/edit?usp=sharing). It has 2 columns: **Text** and **Image**. Each row = one variant. You can use this sheet as-is (with view access) or make a copy for your own data.

## Step 3: Generate Variants

This node loops through each row of data and creates a personalized variant.

**Configuration:**
1. Template ID comes from Step 1 automatically
2. Overrides map sheet columns to your Canva layers: **Text** → text layer, **Image** → image layer (URL)
3. If your Canva layer IDs aren't `text` and `image`, change the layer IDs in the overrides to match your design

**Tip:** Use the layer dropdown to see all available layers in your template.

## Step 4: Output

The "Create Variant" node outputs the rendered image data.

**Next steps:**
You can add more nodes to:
- Download images to local storage
- Upload to cloud storage (S3, Google Drive, Dropbox)
- Send via email or Slack
- Post to social media
- Insert URLs into a database or CRM

meta.name: Create personalized images from Google Sheets data with Layerre
